data.body:
Local validation pass done (step 1, the creds-free portion):

- `make infra-upgrade-terraform-deps` (runs `terraform init -backend=false -upgrade` per root module — no AWS creds) refreshed provider resolution to AWS v6.50.0 across all 5 root modules.
- **Caught a real merge defect:** the textually-clean (marker-free) Copier app-layer merge had duplicated the `enable_sms_notifications` local in `app-config/main.tf` (OSCER already had the SMS block in-tree, so the merge appended an identical copy). Terraform rejected it as "Attribute redefined." Fixed (removed the dup) and folded into the app-layer commit so no broken intermediate commit exists.
- `terraform validate` now passes for accounts, networks, build-repository, database, service under provider v6.50.0 → OSCER's config uses nothing v6 removed.
- Lock files are GITIGNORED in OSCER (`.gitignore:7` + `infra/.gitignore`), so the refresh was a verification artifact, not a committed deliverable — the tracked contract is the `~> 6.50.0` constraint, locks regenerate at deploy. No lock changes to commit.

Branch now: 2 commits (`2accd4a4` base, `c531229d` app), tree clean, every commit valid.

**Still remaining (needs creds / not local):** `terraform plan` per env to see what v6 changes against live state (the real go/no-go); review AWS v6 upgrade guide for OSCER resources beyond the template; reconcile DDE files w/ PR #679; close PR #705; push + open PR (awaiting Mike).
